exercises.prompt: Read the passage and answer the questions.

data.passage: The water cycle is one of the most important processes on Earth, yet most of the action happens invisibly. When the sun heats oceans, lakes, and rivers, water evaporates into the atmosphere as water vapor. As this vapor rises, it cools and condenses around tiny particles of dust or pollen, forming clouds. When cloud droplets combine and grow heavy enough, they fall as precipitation—rain, snow, sleet, or hail depending on temperature. Some precipitation soaks into the ground and becomes groundwater, slowly filtering through soil and rock to feed wells and springs. Some flows over the surface as runoff into streams and rivers, eventually returning to the ocean. Plants also play a role through transpiration, releasing water vapor from their leaves. Scientists estimate that a single water molecule may take thousands of years to complete one full cycle, meaning the water you drink today may have once been part of a glacier during the Ice Age.

questions.question: Based on the passage, why might a drought in one region eventually affect water availability hundreds of miles away?
options: Because water cycles through connected systems like rivers and groundwater
Because droughts cause earthquakes that break water pipes
Because the sun stops heating water during droughts
Because clouds cannot form without rain
questions.correctOption: Because water cycles through connected systems like rivers and groundwater

questions.question: What can you infer about why the author mentions the Ice Age at the end of the passage?
options: To show that water molecules are recycled over extremely long time periods
To prove that ice ages were caused by the water cycle
To explain why glaciers no longer exist
To argue that modern water is different from ancient water
questions.correctOption: To show that water molecules are recycled over extremely long time periods

questions.question: Why does the author describe the water cycle as happening 'invisibly'?
options: Because evaporation and water vapor are not visible to the naked eye
Because water cannot be seen in nature
Because scientists have never observed the water cycle
Because the water cycle only happens at night
questions.correctOption: Because evaporation and water vapor are not visible to the naked eye
